questions.question: Why is the worst case of Quick Sort O(n²) and how can it be avoided?
questions.answer:
Worst Case Occurs When:
- Array already sorted (ascending/descending)
- Always picking smallest/largest as pivot
- Each partition produces one empty and one (n-1) element partition
- Recurrence: T(n) = T(n-1) + O(n) = O(n²)

How to Avoid:
1. Randomized pivot selection
   - Randomly choose pivot index
   - Expected O(n log n)

2. Median-of-three
   - Pivot = median of first, middle, last
   - Avoids worst case for sorted arrays

3. Introsort (hybrid)
   - Switch to heapsort if recursion too deep

With good pivot selection, average case is O(n log n).
